div.p: BPF_MAP_TYPE_PERCPU_ARRAY Entry Size: num_cpus × sizeof(u64) For 8 CPUs: 8 × 8 = 64 bytes per entry Memory per Entry: ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ Offset 0x00: CPU 0 counter (8 bytes) │ │ Offset 0x08: CPU 1 counter (8 bytes) │ │ Offset 0x10: CPU 2 counter (8 bytes) │ │ Offset 0x18: CPU 3 counter (8 bytes) │ │ Offset 0x20: CPU 4 counter (8 bytes) │ │ Offset 0x28: CPU 5 counter (8 bytes) │ │ Offset 0x30: CPU 6 counter (8 bytes) │ │ Offset 0x38: CPU 7 counter (8 bytes) │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────┘

✓ No cache line bouncing Each CPU writes to its own cache line ✓ No atomic instructions Simple memory writes, not RMW operations ✓ Scales with CPU count More CPUs = more parallelism, not more contention ✓ ~100x faster updates vs. atomic increment on contended cache line
